markdown.markdown:
```{r setup, include=FALSE} library(tidyverse) library(lubridate) library(ISOweek) library(leaflet) library(zoo) library(forecast) library(spdep) library(sf) library(dbscan) ``` ```{r} noise <- read.csv("../Data/311_Service_Requests.csv") # List all types and descriptors unique(noise$Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type) unique(noise$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) noise <- noise %>% mutate(Created.Date = mdy_hms(Created.Date), Closed.Date = mdy_hms(Closed.Date), Resolution.Action.Updated.Date = mdy_hms(Resolution.Action.Updated.Date)) # Considering dropping 1.5% of observations: without lat/long, these are marginally more difficult to geocode. noise_ll <- noise %>% filter(!is.na(Latitude)) range(noise_ll$Created.Date) # Date range aligns with SONYC noise_ll <- noise_ll %>% select(Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type., Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., Created.Date, Latitude, Longitude, Location) noise_ll$week <- strftime(noise_ll$Created.Date, format = "%V") %>% as.numeric() noise_ll$month <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y-%m") noise_ll$month <- as.Date(paste0(noise_ll$month, "-01"), format = "%Y-%m-%d") noise_ll$year <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y") noise_weekly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year, week) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_monthly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_yearly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") ``` # Graph Complaints (Weekly/Monthly/Yearly) ```{r} ggplot(noise_weekly, aes(x = interaction(year, week), y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + labs( title = "Weekly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Week", y = "Number of complaints" ) + theme_minimal() ggplot(noise_monthly, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + scale_x_date( date_breaks = "4 months", date_labels = "%Y-%m" ) + labs( title = "Monthly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Month", y = "Number of complaints" ) + theme_minimal() # Highly seasonal with peaks in June and September, troughs in December/January. # Break it down by complaint type to see where this is coming from. ggplot(noise_yearly, aes(x = year, y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + labs( title = "Yearly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Year", y = "Number of complaints" ) + theme_minimal() # Identify week-level anomalies (rolling z-score) noise_weekly <- noise_weekly %>% arrange(year, week) %>% mutate( roll_mean = rollmean(total_complaints, 8, fill = NA, align = "right"), roll_sd = rollapply(total_complaints, 8, sd, fill = NA, align = "right"), z = (total_complaints - roll_mean) / roll_sd, spike = z > 2, date = ISOweek2date(paste0(year, "-W", sprintf("%02d", week), "-1")) ) ggplot(noise_weekly, aes(x = date, y = total_complaints)) + geom_line() + geom_line(aes(y = roll_mean), linetype = "dashed") + geom_point(data = subset(noise_weekly, spike), color = "red") + labs( title = "Weekly Street Noise Complaint Counts with Spikes Highlighted", x = "Week", y = "Number of complaints" ) + theme_minimal() # No hotspots identified at the 4- or 6-week levels. # Using an 8-week average, 6 hotspots were identified, each between February 20th and April 30th. ``` # Monthly seasonality tests for all types ```{r} noise_monthly_type <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") count(noise_ll, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% mutate(`%` = round(n/sum(n) * 100)) %>% arrange(desc(n)) ggplot(noise_monthly_type, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints)) + geom_line() + facet_wrap(~ Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., scales = "free_y") + labs( title = "Monthly Noise Complaints by Type", x = "Month", y = "Total Complaints" ) + theme_minimal() # The pattern we saw above is largely driven by Loud Music/Party, which accounts for 39% of all complaints. # However, similar patterns are also seen in Car/Truck Music (11%), Loud Talking (13%), and Noise, Ice Cream Truck (1%). # This breakdown shows that 64% of complaints belong to summer-seasonal categories. While Car/Truck Horn, Engine Idling, # Alarms, and Construction-related categories (those potentially closer to siren sounds) don't show seasonality, # most noise complaints filed on the street are more common in June and September. ``` # Monthly seasonality checks from SONYC (and 311 dashboard) ```{r} # Create a timeseries object noise_ts <- ts(noise_weekly$total_complaints, frequency = 52, start = c(2016,11)) # Automatically assign best ARIMA model using AIC fit_arima <- auto.arima(noise_ts, seasonal = TRUE) summary(fit_arima) # Best ARIMA model incorporates seasonality. # Separate trend from seasonality and check for a pattern. decomp <- stl(noise_ts, s.window = "periodic") plot(decomp) # Clear seasonal pattern! # Compare this to the models recommended by 311 dashboard for related complaints fit_naive <- naive(noise_ts) fit_tbats <- tbats(noise_ts) # All models' residuals pass Ljung-Box test: no pattern suggesting the model is missing something systematically. checkresiduals(fit_naive) checkresiduals(fit_arima) checkresiduals(fit_tbats) # Cross-validate models and compare MSE e_arima <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { forecast(auto.arima(y), h = h) }) e_naive <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { forecast(naive(y), h = h) }) e_tbats <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { forecast(tbats(y), h = h) }) sprintf("ARIMA MSE: %s", format(mean(e_arima^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) sprintf("Naive MSE: %s", format(mean(e_naive^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) sprintf("TBATS MSE: %s", format(mean(e_tbats^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # Naive model is the best predictor in terms of MSE. ``` # Map Noise Complaints by NTA ```{r} # Too many complaints to look for clusters like we did with SONYC. Let's aggregate complaints per capita at the NTA level. noise_sf <- st_as_sf(noise_ll, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) # Read in demographics data from councilcount, replace likely population flukes with 0. ntas <- st_read("../Data/nta-demo.geojson") %>% st_transform(st_crs(noise_sf)) %>% select(nta, total_popE, geometry) %>% mutate(total_popE = if_else(total_popE < 100, 0, total_popE)) noise_with_nta <- st_join(noise_sf, ntas, join = st_within) nta_counts <- noise_with_nta %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(nta) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") nta_map <- ntas %>% left_join(nta_counts, by = "nta") %>% mutate( total_complaints = coalesce(total_complaints, 0), complaints_pc = case_when( total_complaints == 0 ~ 0, total_popE == 0 ~ NA_real_, TRUE ~ (total_complaints / total_popE) * 1000 ) ) pal <- colorNumeric( rev(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "RdYlGn")), domain = sqrt(nta_map$total_complaints) ) pal_pc <- colorNumeric( rev(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "RdYlGn")), domain = sqrt(nta_map$complaints_pc)) # Map raw complaints leaflet(nta_map) %>% addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~pal(sqrt(total_complaints)), fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints: ", format(total_complaints, big.mark = ",") ) ) %>% addLegend( "bottomright", pal = pal, values = ~sqrt(total_complaints), title = "Square Root of Total Complaints" ) # Total complaints are highest in Hillcrest and Washington Heights. # Upper Manhattan, Harlem, Concourse/Mt. Eden, Midtown/Chelsea, and North-Central Brooklyn are other hotspots. # Map complaints per capita leaflet(nta_map) %>% addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~pal_pc(sqrt(complaints_pc)), fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints per 1k Residents: ", round(complaints_pc) ) ) %>% addLegend( "bottomright", pal = pal_pc, values = ~sqrt(complaints_pc), title = "Square Root of Complaints per 1k Residents" ) # Hillcrest remains dominant after controlling for population. # Midtown, Harlem, and Upper Manhattan remain hotspots. SoHo/Tribeca are more notable at this level too. # Concourse/Mt Eden and North-Central Brooklyn still see more complaints than their neighbors, but stand out less. ``` # Test for hotspots ```{r} # Collapse complaints with duplicate locations for cluster analysis noise_collapsed <- noise_sf %>% group_by(Location) %>% summarise( total_obs = n(), .groups = "drop" ) # Project for distance-based neighbors (meters) noise_collapsed_proj <- st_transform(noise_collapsed, 32618) # UTM zone 18N for NYC coords <- st_coordinates(noise_collapsed_proj) # Define neighbors within 500 m neighbors <- dnearneigh(coords, 0, 500) weights <- nb2listw(neighbors, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE) gi_star <- localG(noise_collapsed_proj$total_obs, listw = weights, zero.policy = TRUE) noise_collapsed_proj$GiZ <- as.numeric(gi_star) noise_collapsed_leaflet <- st_transform(noise_collapsed_proj, 4326) # back to WGS84 noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers <- noise_collapsed_leaflet %>% filter(abs(GiZ) > 2.58) # "Narrows down" to 12k coordinate pairs identified as hotspots in a 500m radius (significant at 1% level) pal <- colorNumeric("RdYlBu", domain = noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers$GiZ, reverse = TRUE) leaflet(noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers) %>% addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% setView(lng = -74.0, lat = 40.7, zoom = 10) %>% addCircleMarkers( radius = 5, color = ~pal(GiZ), fillOpacity = 0.8, stroke = FALSE, popup = ~paste0( "Gi*: ", round(GiZ, 2), "
", "Total Obs: ", total_obs ) ) %>% addLegend("bottomright", pal = pal, values = ~GiZ, title = "Getis-Ord Gi* Z") # This visual came out much better than expected. # The same Queens NTA remains the biggest outlier and clusters are also seen in Upper Manhattan/Harlem/Concourse. # Midtown/Tribeca/Soho get their own clusters, and the North-Central Brooklyn group sees pinpoints in Williamsburg and Prospect Lefferts Gardens. # Fox Hills also emerges as the complaint hotspot of Staten Island, which was easier to miss at the NTA level. ```

List all types and descriptors unique(noise$Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type) unique(noise$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) noise <- noise %>% mutate(Created.Date = mdy_hms(Created.Date), Closed.Date = mdy_hms(Closed.Date), Resolution.Action.Updated.Date = mdy_hms(Resolution.Action.Updated.Date)) # Considering dropping 1.5% of observations: without lat/long, these are marginally more difficult to geocode. noise_ll <- noise %>% filter(!is.na(Latitude)) range(noise_ll$Created.Date) # Date range aligns with SONYC noise_ll <- noise_ll %>% select(Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type., Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., Created.Date, Latitude, Longitude, Location) noise_ll$week <- strftime(noise_ll$Created.Date, format = "%V") %>% as.numeric() noise_ll$month <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y-%m") noise_ll$month <- as.Date(paste0(noise_ll$month, "-01"), format = "%Y-%m-%d") noise_ll$year <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y") noise_weekly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year, week) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_monthly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_yearly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") ``` # The pattern we saw above is largely driven by Loud Music/Party, which accounts for 39% of all complaints. # However, similar patterns are also seen in Car/Truck Music (11%), Loud Talking (13%), and Noise, Ice Cream Truck (1%). # This breakdown shows that 64% of complaints belong to summer-seasonal categories. While Car/Truck Horn, Engine Idling, # Alarms, and Construction-related categories (those potentially closer to siren sounds) don't show seasonality, # most noise complaints filed on the street are more common in June and September. ``` # Monthly seasonality checks from SONYC (and 311 dashboard) ```{r} # Create a timeseries object noise_ts <- ts(noise_weekly$total_complaints, frequency = 52, start = c(2016,11)) # Automatically assign best ARIMA model using AIC fit_arima <- auto.arima(noise_ts, seasonal = TRUE) summary(fit_arima) # Best ARIMA model incorporates seasonality. # Separate trend from seasonality and check for a pattern. decomp <- stl(noise_ts, s.window = "periodic") plot(decomp) # Clear seasonal pattern! # Compare this to the models recommended by 311 dashboard for related complaints fit_naive <- naive(noise_ts) fit_tbats <- tbats(noise_ts) # All models' residuals pass Ljung-Box test: no pattern suggesting the model is missing something systematically. checkresiduals(fit_naive) checkresiduals(fit_arima) checkresiduals(fit_tbats) # Cross-validate models and compare MSE e_arima <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { forecast(auto.arima(y), h = h) }) e_naive <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { forecast(naive(y), h = h) }) e_tbats <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { forecast(tbats(y), h = h) }) sprintf("ARIMA MSE: %s", format(mean(e_arima^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) sprintf("Naive MSE: %s", format(mean(e_naive^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) sprintf("TBATS MSE: %s", format(mean(e_tbats^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # Naive model is the best predictor in terms of MSE. ``` # Map Noise Complaints by NTA ```{r} # Too many complaints to look for clusters like we did with SONYC. language.appendix-attribution-cite-as: For attribution, please cite this work as:
